title: Living in the Shadows: One Family's Endless Battle With ICE Panic
content:
Irvi's unresolved immigration status trailed them, usually from a distance. ICE agents once entered their apartment building looking for a neighbor. Rachel stood guard at the peephole while Irvi hid in the bedroom. A few years later, Irvi borrowed his in-laws' car to take the girls to Coney Island. As they approached the park, he spotted a police checkpoint at the entrance.

He pulled over and had a panic attack, then tried to play it off by telling the girls that the car was breaking down. Rachel and Irvi talked about what to do if ICE ever tried to arrest him while she was at work. Irvi would have to persuade the officers to wait long enough for family to pick up the girls. For years, Congress debated changes to the immigration system, including ones that would have helped Irvi. When Sara was old enough, she joined Rachel at protests to fight for her father's right to stay.

Rachel rode a bus to Washington, D.C., where members of Congress admitted they hadn't known so many mixed-status families were in limbo. Up to this point, their daughters only had a vague understanding of the danger. Ana lost interest in playing with other children and suffered from nightmares and panic attacks. Irvi tried to distract her with bedtime stories about a hero who fought monsters inspired by rude customers at his restaurant. 

During the summer of 2024, a new program offered hope that people like Irvi could become citizens through an American spouse. Rachel submitted an application with hundreds of documents illustrating the life they had built together. However, a federal judge soon put the program on hold, and it was later terminated completely. The constant fear of deportation forced the family to make a desperate choice to flee.

They crammed their belongings into an SUV and began a tense three-day drive. The journey was smooth until they arrived at a gas station swarming with Border Patrol agents. Irvi started panicking, and his face went gray with fear. They had to constantly keep moving, guided by Irvi's nervous system screaming at him to get out of the area.
